channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Volunteer with the Sidewalk Warriors!
item.description: On Thursday, April 2, 2026, beginning at 4:30 PM, the CCPD and participating students and alumni will be volunteering with Sidewalk Warriors in Troy, New York. See below for information on how you can volunteer! Register in Handshake: https://rpi.joinhandshake.com/events/1890471/share_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Still Accepting Applications for CSTEP!
item.description: The Collegiate Science & Technology Entry Program (CSTEP) is excited to announce that applications for the Spring semester are now open! CSTEP is an academic enrichment program designed to support New York State residents who are economically disadvantaged and pursuing careers in STEM or health-related fields.
item.author: Students

item.title: Cap & Gown Information
item.description: Cap & Gown Information can be found here: https://administration.rpi.edu/collegiate-store/commencement Deadlines: Bachelor or Master Degrees: If you wish to participate in the early May pickup event order online no later than Friday, March 27, 2026. If you want the regalia shipped to you, order online no later than Thursday April 9, 2026. Doctorate Degrees: Friday, March 20, 2026 For additional questions, reach out to: RPI@bkstr.com
item.author: Students

item.title: OGE Seeking Nominations for Faculty Advising Award
item.description: The Office of Graduate Education is seeking nominations for the Faculty Advising Award for Graduate Education. The award recognizes faculty members whose advising has had a meaningful and lasting impact on graduate students. Nomination letters (max. 2 pages) should provide specific examples of excellence in mentoring and student development, fostering a positive and productive research or academic environment, and the overall impact of the nominee’s advising. Submit to madige@rpi.edu by April 1.
item.author: Community

item.title: Student Experience Excellence Awards
item.description: The Student Experience Excellence Awards (formerly the Graduating Student Awards) recognize RPI students who have made exceptional contributions to our community through academic achievement, athletic accomplishment, leadership, service, and/or volunteerism. We invite students, staff, and faculty to nominate individuals who exemplify excellence and have made a meaningful impact on our campus.
item.author: Community

item.title: Rπ Giving Day
item.description: Date: Saturday, March 14, 2026 (all day) - Join us in support of RPI students, teams and student-athletes, clubs and activities, fraternities and sororities, scholarships, schools, and our area of greatest need – the Rensselaer Annual Fund. The day will also feature a fun Rπ Giving Day πK run. Visit the website to learn how you can get involved!
item.author: Community

item.title: A Non-Variational Quantum Approach to Solving Combinatorial Optimization Problems: Iterative-QAOA
item.description: Date: Tuesday, March 17, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Quantum computers introduce new opportunities for modeling and solving decision problems. In this talk, we will first discuss the unique positioning of Industrial Engineering in the quantum research landscape that motivates new directions at the intersection of application and theory. Then as an example, we will present a new algorithm, Iterative-QAOA, for combinational optimization problems.
item.author: Community

item.title: Success Center Tutors Hosting Review Sessions
item.description: Cal II: Tuesday, 3/10 7-9pm in the Nason Classroom ENGR Dynamics: Thursday, 3/12 7-9pm at the Success Center
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Security - PhishStick Security Analyst
item.description: Our Information Security team has deployed a Security Analyst tool as part of our AI Phishing Coach: PhishStick. PhishStick will assist with protecting our RPI Community from advanced email-based Phishing attacks. Please click above link for more information regarding reporting phishing messages to Microsoft, and features of our PhishStick Security Analyst.
item.author: Community

item.title: City of Troy Street Cleaning Operations Underway
item.description: The City of Troy has commenced its seasonal street cleaning operations. Residents and visitors are strongly encouraged to observe all posted parking signs to avoid citations and vehicle towing.
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “Getting More Done Without Burning Out”
item.description:
Imagine sprinting through a marathon of endless demands—where every step forward risks burnout unless you rethink your approach. Traditional productivity strategies can backfire, eroding focus, energy, and decision-making. Unlock sustainable productivity in Nabeela Elsayed’s Getting More Done Without Burning Out Leadercamp, and discover strategies to stay composed, focus on what matters, and inspire your team with clarity and confidence. Two times: 9:00A-10:00A & 12:00PM-1:00PM.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
